title: IISc Administrative Assistant Guess Paper 9
subtitle: Hard-tier prediction set modelled on 2023 examiner patterns
instructions: Total 80 questions to be attempted in 90 minutes.
Negative marking applies: 0.33333 marks (one-third) are deducted for every incorrect answer.
Figure-based placeholder questions are unscored and carry 0 marks either way.
Difficulty is benchmarked at or above the actual 2023 paper: expect multi-step questions.

questions.text: Two numbers are respectively 30% and 40% less than a third number. The first number is what percentage of the second?
A man sells an article for Rs 96 and gains a percentage numerically equal to its cost price in rupees. The cost price is:
questions.explanation: CP + CP^2/100 = 96 gives CP = 60.

questions.text: An article marked at Rs 2000 is sold after successive discounts of 10%, 20% and 25%. The selling price is:
12 men or 18 women can complete a work in 14 days. In how many days will 8 men and 16 women complete the same work?
questions.explanation: 8 men = 12 women, so 28 women; 18 x 14 / 28 = 9.

questions.text: Two pipes can fill a cistern in 15 and 20 minutes. Both are opened together, but the first is turned off after 4 minutes. The total time to fill the cistern is:
questions.explanation: 4 minutes fill 7/15; the rest takes (8/15)x20 = 32/3 min more.

questions.text: Without stoppages a bus averages 54 km/h; with stoppages it averages 45 km/h. For how many minutes per hour does the bus stop?
questions.explanation: It loses 9 of 54 km, i.e. 1/6 of the hour = 10 minutes.

questions.text: A train crosses a 120 m platform in 15 seconds and a pole in 9 seconds. The length of the train is:
On a sum at 8% per annum compound interest, the interest for the second year exceeds that for the first year by Rs 64. The sum is:
questions.explanation: The excess is 8% of the first year's interest: 0.0064P = 64.

questions.text: The mean proportional between 0.08 and 0.18 is:
A merchant has 100 kg of sugar. He sells part of it at 7% profit and the rest at 17% profit, gaining 10% overall. The quantity sold at 17% profit is:
questions.explanation: Alligation gives 7:3, so 30 kg at 17%.

questions.text: Enter the count of numbers between 100 and 500 that are divisible by 7.
If a + b + c = 9 and ab + bc + ca = 26, then a^2 + b^2 + c^2 equals:
A, B and C rent a pasture. A grazes 10 oxen for 7 months, B grazes 12 oxen for 5 months and C grazes 15 oxen for 3 months. If the rent is Rs 175, C's share is:
questions.explanation: Oxen-months 70:60:45 total 175; C pays 45.

questions.text: A solid metal sphere of radius 3 cm is melted and drawn into a wire of radius 0.1 cm. The length of the wire is:
questions.explanation: 36 pi = pi x 0.01 x L gives L = 3600 cm.

questions.text: The area of a triangle with sides 13 cm, 14 cm and 15 cm is:
A triangle has vertices at (0,0), (6,0) and (0,8). Its perimeter is:
questions.explanation: Sides 6, 8 and 10 give perimeter 24.

questions.text: Sentence 1: Artificial lighting has stretched the human day far beyond sunset. Arrange P, Q, R, S. P: Sleep researchers link this exposure to rising insomnia and fatigue. Q: Blue light from screens delays the release of melatonin at night. R: Experts therefore advise switching off devices an hour before bed. S: The body's internal clock, tuned for darkness, is easily confused.
questions.explanation: Vulnerability, mechanism, evidence, advice.

questions.text: Sentence 1: The bicycle is enjoying a global revival. Arrange P, Q, R, S. P: Cities from Paris to Pune are laying dedicated cycle lanes. Q: Congested roads and climate worries have pushed commuters to rethink cars. R: Health benefits add a further argument in the bicycle's favour. S: Planners see cycling as the cheapest cure for urban gridlock.
questions.explanation: Causes, planners' view, action taken, extra argument.

questions.text: Select the meaning of the idiom: To have too many irons in the fire

options.text: To be involved in too many activities at once
To be well prepared for danger

questions.explanation: It warns against juggling too many ventures.

questions.text: Select the meaning of the idiom: To fish in troubled waters

options.text: To take advantage of a disturbed situation

questions.explanation: It means profiting from others' troubles.

questions.text: Select the meaning of the idiom: To blow one's own trumpet
questions.explanation: It means boasting about oneself.

questions.text: A person or thing that cannot be corrected or reformed:
questions.explanation: Incorrigible means beyond correction.

questions.text: A work published after the death of its author:
questions.explanation: Posthumous works appear after the author's death.

questions.text: Select the correctly spelt word:
questions.explanation: It ends in -crasy, not -cracy.

questions.text: Select the incorrectly spelt word:
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is commission.

questions.text: Improve the underlined part: MANY A STUDENTS HAVE passed this examination with distinction.
questions.explanation: 'Many a' takes a singular noun and verb.

questions.text: Improve the underlined part: He denied THAT HE HAS stolen the documents.
questions.explanation: Past reporting verb shifts the clause to past perfect.

questions.text: Select the synonym of PRECARIOUS:
questions.explanation: Precarious means insecure or unstable.

questions.text: Select the antonym of ZENITH:
questions.explanation: Zenith (highest point) is opposite to nadir.

questions.passage: More than a hundred million fragments of debris now orbit the Earth, from dead satellites to flecks of paint travelling ten times faster than a bullet. At such speeds even a centimetre-sized shard can disable a spacecraft. Every collision spawns thousands of new fragments, raising the odds of further impacts, a chain reaction known as the Kessler syndrome that could one day render key orbits unusable. Space agencies now require new satellites to deorbit within years of retirement, and startups are testing nets, harpoons and robotic arms to capture large wrecks. Cleaning up, engineers concede, will be far harder than not littering in the first place.
questions.text: According to the passage, tiny debris fragments are dangerous because:

options.text: They travel at extremely high speeds

questions.explanation: Speed, not size, gives them destructive energy.

questions.passage: More than a hundred million fragments of debris now orbit the Earth, from dead satellites to flecks of paint travelling ten times faster than a bullet. At such speeds even a centimetre-sized shard can disable a spacecraft. Every collision spawns thousands of new fragments, raising the odds of further impacts, a chain reaction known as the Kessler syndrome that could one day render key orbits unusable. Space agencies now require new satellites to deorbit within years of retirement, and startups are testing nets, harpoons and robotic arms to capture large wrecks. Cleaning up, engineers concede, will be far harder than not littering in the first place.
questions.text: The 'Kessler syndrome' described in the passage refers to:

options.text: A cascade of collisions that multiplies debris

questions.explanation: Each collision breeds fragments that cause more collisions.

questions.passage: More than a hundred million fragments of debris now orbit the Earth, from dead satellites to flecks of paint travelling ten times faster than a bullet. At such speeds even a centimetre-sized shard can disable a spacecraft. Every collision spawns thousands of new fragments, raising the odds of further impacts, a chain reaction known as the Kessler syndrome that could one day render key orbits unusable. Space agencies now require new satellites to deorbit within years of retirement, and startups are testing nets, harpoons and robotic arms to capture large wrecks. Cleaning up, engineers concede, will be far harder than not littering in the first place.
questions.text: The final sentence implies that the most effective strategy against space debris is:

options.text: Preventing new debris rather than removing old debris

questions.explanation: Not littering is said to beat cleaning up.

questions.text: Find the next term: 3, 5, 9, 17, 33, ?
questions.explanation: Each term is double the previous minus 1.

questions.text: Find the next term: 100, 50, 52, 26, 28, ?
questions.explanation: The pattern alternates: divide by 2, add 2.

questions.text: Find the next term: 0, 6, 24, 60, 120, ?
Find the next term: DGK, GJN, JMQ, ?
questions.explanation: Each position advances 3 letters between terms.

questions.text: How many meaningful English words can be formed using the letters E, A, T, each exactly once?
questions.explanation: EAT, TEA and ATE can all be formed.

questions.text: If SHARP is coded as QSBIT, how is TABLE coded?
questions.explanation: Reverse the word, then shift each letter +1.

questions.text: If WATER = 67, then EARTH = ?
If 'sky' is called 'sea', 'sea' is called 'water', 'water' is called 'air' and 'air' is called 'cloud', then fish live in what (in this language)?
questions.explanation: Fish live in water, and water is called 'air'.

questions.text: Seven people P, Q, R, S, T, U, V sit in a row facing north. V is at the extreme right and T at the extreme left. Q is second from the left and S is exactly in the middle. P sits between Q and S. R is immediately to the right of S, and U sits between R and V. Who is third from the right end?
questions.explanation: Order: T, Q, P, S, R, U, V; third from right is R.

questions.text: Eight friends sit around a circular table: B between A and C, D between C and E, F between E and G, H between G and A. Who are the immediate neighbours of H?
questions.explanation: The order is A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H around the circle.

questions.text: Raju is 11th from both ends of his class ranking. How many students are in the class?
How many days are there from 15 March to 15 August of the same (non-leap) year, counting 15 August but not 15 March?
Looking at a portrait, a man said, 'I have no brothers and sisters, but that man's father is my father's son.' Whose portrait was it?
questions.explanation: 'My father's son' is the speaker himself, so the man is his son.

questions.text: P + Q means P is the sister of Q; P x Q means P is the son of Q; P / Q means P is the father of Q. Which expression shows that A is the grandfather of B?
questions.explanation: A is father of C and C is father of B.

questions.text: Ram is facing west. He turns 45 degrees clockwise, then 180 degrees, and finally 270 degrees anticlockwise. Which direction is he facing now?
questions.explanation: 270 -> 315 -> 135 -> 225 degrees, which is south-west.

questions.text: If P denotes 'x', Q denotes '/', R denotes '+' and S denotes '-', then the value of 18 Q 6 P 4 R 9 S 5 is:
ACEG : ZXVT :: BDFH : ?
questions.explanation: Each letter maps to its reverse-alphabet partner.

questions.text: Find the odd one out: BDF, HJL, NPR, TVX, ZAC
questions.explanation: All others rise by 2 within the trio; ZAC breaks the step.

questions.text: Statements: Some doctors are engineers. All engineers are pilots. Conclusions: I. Some pilots are doctors. II. Some pilots are engineers.
questions.explanation: Doctor-engineers are pilots, and engineers are pilots.

questions.text: Select the figure that continues the series, where the arrow rotates 45 degrees clockwise and a dot alternates between two corners.

figure.label: Figural Series
figure.description: A four-figure sequence combining a rotating arrow with an alternating dot position; choose the fifth figure.

questions.explanation: Unscored placeholder for a visual series question.

questions.text: Two positions of a dice with letters A to F on its faces are shown. Determine the letter opposite A.

figure.label: Dice Positions (Letters)
figure.description: Two views of a lettered dice from which the face opposite A must be deduced.

questions.explanation: Unscored placeholder for a visual dice question.

questions.text: The ICC Champions Trophy 2025 was officially hosted by which country, with India playing its matches in Dubai under a hybrid model?
questions.explanation: Pakistan hosted; India's games were shifted to Dubai.

questions.text: The 58th Jnanpith Award (announced 2024) was conferred jointly on Gulzar and:
questions.explanation: Sanskrit scholar Jagadguru Rambhadracharya shared it.

questions.text: 'Astra', developed by DRDO, is what type of missile?
questions.explanation: Astra is a BVR air-to-air missile.

questions.text: The Account Aggregator framework, which lets customers share financial data with consent, is regulated by:
questions.explanation: AAs are NBFCs licensed by the RBI.

questions.text: The Doctrine of Lapse, used to annex states like Jhansi and Satara, is associated with:
questions.explanation: Dalhousie applied the doctrine in the 1840s-50s.

questions.text: The murals of the Ajanta caves primarily depict:

options.text: Jataka tales of the Buddha's lives

questions.explanation: Ajanta's Buddhist art narrates the Jatakas.

questions.text: The Duncan Passage separates:
questions.explanation: It lies between South Andaman and Little Andaman.

questions.text: The Sela Tunnel, inaugurated in 2024 to give all-weather access towards Tawang, is located in:
questions.explanation: It crosses the Sela Pass in Arunachal Pradesh.

questions.text: Kutiyattam, recognised by UNESCO as a masterpiece of intangible heritage, is a Sanskrit theatre tradition of:
questions.explanation: Kutiyattam is performed in Kerala's temple theatres.

questions.text: The Vice-President of India is the ex-officio:
questions.explanation: Article 64 makes the VP the Rajya Sabha Chairman.

questions.text: 'Poverty and Un-British Rule in India', which propounded the Drain Theory, was written by:
questions.explanation: Naoroji quantified the colonial drain of wealth.

questions.text: The Gwadar Port, developed with Chinese assistance under CPEC, is located in:
questions.explanation: Gwadar is in Pakistan's Balochistan province.

questions.text: India's medal tally at the Hangzhou Asian Games (2023) crossed 100 for the first time, finishing at:
questions.explanation: India ended with 107 medals, including 28 golds.

questions.text: The RBI-DPI, published half-yearly by the Reserve Bank, is an index that measures:

options.text: The extent of digital payments adoption

questions.explanation: The Digital Payments Index tracks payment digitisation.

questions.text: 'Mission Divyastra' (March 2024) was the first flight test of which missile with MIRV technology?
questions.explanation: Agni-V flew with multiple independently targetable warheads.

questions.text: The 'Namo Drone Didi' scheme provides drones to:

options.text: Women self-help groups for agricultural services

questions.explanation: SHG women rent out drones for spraying and monitoring.

questions.text: In Excel, what does =IF(AND(5>3, 2>4), "A", "B") return?
questions.explanation: AND is FALSE because 2>4 fails.

questions.text: In Excel, the operator used to join two text strings (e.g. ="IN"&"DIA") is:
questions.explanation: The ampersand concatenates text.

questions.text: In MS Word, Ctrl + Enter inserts:
questions.explanation: Ctrl + Enter forces a new page.

questions.text: In Excel, Ctrl + Page Down moves the focus to:

options.text: The last row of the sheet
The bottom of the current column

questions.explanation: It switches to the next sheet tab.

questions.text: Registers, the fastest storage elements, are located:
questions.explanation: Registers sit within the processor itself.

questions.text: EEPROM differs from ordinary ROM in that it:

options.text: Loses data when power is off
Can be erased and reprogrammed electrically

questions.explanation: EEPROM is electrically erasable and rewritable.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a function of an operating system?
questions.explanation: Compilation is done by a compiler, not the OS.

questions.text: A deadlock occurs when:

options.text: Two or more processes wait indefinitely for resources held by each other
A program has an infinite loop

questions.explanation: Circular waiting on resources causes deadlock.

questions.text: In PowerPoint, Shift + F5 starts the slide show:
questions.explanation: F5 starts from the beginning; Shift+F5 from the current slide.

questions.text: A 'nibble' consists of how many bits?
questions.explanation: A nibble is half a byte: 4 bits.
